sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Treasury withdrawal to fund 12 months of Eternl operations, maintenance, development, support, audits, and administration from August 2026 to July 2027.

Deliverables: Frontend maintenance and cross-platform development for web, browser extension, Android, and iOS, Backend infrastructure and operations including servers, Cardano nodes, DBSync/indexers, application services, monitoring, alerting, and incident response, User support through Discord, Telegram, public channels, ecosystem project support, wiki updates, and educational videos, Governance tooling enhancements including DRep dashboards, proposal browsers, in-wallet voting, and proposal creation, Eternl Core rewrite, Eternl Hub, enhanced hardware wallet support, and enhanced wallet data export, Periodic independent audits of treasury fund use and oversight metrics
Deadline/expiry: 12-month delivery period, August 2026 to July 2027

Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Eternl is a non-custodial Cardano light wallet for web, browser extension, Android, and iOS used for payments, staking, governance, and DApp interaction.
Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal requests ₳2,350,000, valued at about $0.1787 per Ada, to cover an annual operating cost of about $420,000.
Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal funds 6.0 FTE distributed across 10 contributors at $70,000 per FTE.

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Eternl plans to introduce Pro plans priced at $96 per year for personal users and $360 per year for companies, and estimates that about 5,500 users, or 4.2% of its install base, would cover the $420,000 annual cost.
Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Eternl reports an install base of about 100,000 browser-extension users and about 30,000 Android and iOS users.

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): After withdrawal, the proposer says it will convert up to $420,000 worth of Ada into stablecoins, deposit it into a public company wallet, and return any Ada above that amount to the treasury.
missing: Independent evidence for: Eternl is a non-custodial Cardano light wallet for web, browser extension, Android, and iOS used for payments, staking, governance, and DApp interaction.
Independent evidence for: Eternl reports an install base of about 100,000 browser-extension users and about 30,000 Android and iOS users.
Independent evidence for: The proposer states that 10-18% of Mainnet transactions are conducted via Eternl.

Independent evidence for: The proposer states that without treasury funding, insufficient license sales beyond August could require developer layoffs, scaling Eternl down to essential maintenance, shifting focus outside Cardano, and possibly removing the free option.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: stressed (basis: total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The proposal requests ₳2,350,000, valued at about $0.1787 per Ada, to cover an annual operating cost of about $420,000." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "Eternl is a non-custodial Cardano light wallet for web, browser extension, Android, and iOS used for payments, staking, governance, and DApp interaction." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
